name: Race Car (simpleFoam)
description: External aerodynamics around a race car with ground effect. Uses snappyHexMesh for body-fitted meshing and simpleFoam for steady-state RANS simulation. Features a moving ground plane to simulate road motion and captures downforce-critical underbody flow.
learning_objectives: Copy and prepare surface geometry (STL/OBJ format)
Refine mesh around geometry with snappyHexMesh
Configure moving ground plane for road simulation
Post-process drag and downforce coefficients with forceCoeffs

mesh.title: Generate Mesh
mesh.description: Three-step meshing: surfaceFeatureExtract detects sharp edges, blockMesh creates the background hex mesh, then snappyHexMesh refines around the car geometry with extra refinement near the ground plane for underbody flow.
mesh.tip: Ensure sufficient refinement between the car underbody and ground plane (at least 4-5 cells) to resolve the ground effect properly.

boundaries.title: Set Boundary Conditions
boundaries.description: Configure inlet velocity at 40 m/s, outlet pressure, moving ground (lowerWall), and no-slip walls for the car surfaces. The ground uses movingWallVelocity to match freestream speed.
boundaries.tip: The lowerWall uses movingWallVelocity at freestream speed (40 m/s) to eliminate the ground boundary layer and simulate real road conditions.

solver.title: Configure Solver
solver.description: Set up simpleFoam with SIMPLE algorithm. This is a steady-state solver — endTime controls the maximum number of iterations.
solver.tip: For a quick test run, reduce endTime to 200 iterations. Full convergence typically needs 500+ iterations due to the complex wake structure.

run.title: Run Simulation
run.description: Execute simpleFoam. Watch residuals — all fields should decrease below 1e-4 for convergence. Monitor force coefficients for convergence of drag and downforce.
run.tip: Force coefficients may take 300+ iterations to stabilize. Check both residuals and Cd/Cl convergence before stopping.

postprocess.title: View Results
postprocess.description: Visualize pressure distribution on the car surface, velocity field in the underbody region, and wake structure. Check forceCoeffs output for drag and downforce values (negative Cl = downforce).
postprocess.tip: In ParaView: apply 'Extract Block' to select car surfaces, then color by pressure. Use a cutting plane at y=0 to visualize the symmetry plane flow field.
